name: Bruce Springsteen
abstract: Bruce Frederick Joseph Springsteen (born September 23, 1949) is an American singer, songwriter, and musician. Nicknamed "the Boss", Springsteen has released 21 studio albums spanning six decades; most feature the E Street Band, his backing band since 1972.

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: I think of in the 80s even Madison Square Garden, Giants Stadium, pop stars would fill — you know Billy Joel to Bruce Springsteen, Madonna, Prince, Michael Jackson, © Fillius Jazz Archive -2- whoever, would fill 50,000 seat stadiums regularly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Did you happen to watch the thing the other day, Quincy Jones trying to teach Bruce Springsteen and the poet/singer, what the hell’s his name, Dylan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: This one always comes to mind, on “Born to Run” the Bruce Springsteen record. It was me, Mike and Sanborn and a great trombone player, Wayne Andre.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: He wrote – this is one of the great writers and singers. He sang a song, and he’s been under great criticism. James Zimmerman: Bruce Springsteen.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: I mean it goes from Bruce Springsteen to Elvis Costello and I think these people are frauds.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Calello
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote the strings on “Jungleland.” ... And I met with Bruce.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025
